research_topics: Feature importance for signal selection (XGBoost SHAP)
Kelly criterion for optimal bet sizing

findings.finding: Information Coefficient (IC) measures correlation between signal prediction and actual returns. Grinold rule: Alpha = Volatility * IC * Score. Most equity factors have IC 0.05-0.15. Multi-factor IC typically 0.12-0.18. Alpha decays - most factors lose power by 9 months. Current system uses equal weighting with accuracy gate - IC-based weighting would weight signals by predictive power per horizon.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Our signals range from 31% to 75% accuracy. Equal weighting (with accuracy gate) means a 74% signal (funding@3h) gets same vote as a 52% signal (volume). IC-based weighting would dramatically improve ensemble quality by upweighting proven signals.
findings.source: Grinold 1994, MSCI Barra alpha conversion framework

findings.finding: WFO trains on rolling window, validates on next period, walks forward. Prevents overfitting vs static backtest. Recent paper (arxiv 2602.10785) shows double out-of-sample WFO on crypto. QuantConnect has production WFO API. Key: retrain periodically but not too frequently - weekly or bi-weekly sweet spot.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Our signal weights are static. Walk-forward reweighting on 7-day rolling accuracy would adapt to changing market regimes. Already have accuracy_cache infrastructure to support this.

findings.finding: Hidden Markov Models detect latent market regimes (trending/ranging/volatile). Typical 2-3 state model using returns + volatility. 1-3 day lag for regime shifts. Crypto-specific HMM paper shows BTC regime detection 2024-2026. Regime-switching factor investing improves risk-adjusted returns.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM. We already have regime detection (ranging vs trending via ADX/volatility) but it is rule-based. HMM would be probabilistic, smoother transitions, and can condition signal weights on regime. Trend signal is 40.3% at 3h in current (ranging) regime - HMM could disable it dynamically.

findings.finding: TradingAgents v0.2.4 (released 2026-04-25): LangGraph-based framework with ~12 agents simulating a trading firm. Key innovation: bull vs bear researchers debate with multiple rounds, then risk management team adjudicates. Backtested 23-27% cumulative return on AAPL/GOOGL/AMZN.
findings.relevance_to_us: LOW-MEDIUM. Our Layer 2 is single-agent Claude. Adding a structured debate would improve decision quality but significantly increase latency and cost. Could be implemented as a prompt engineering approach rather than separate agents.

findings.finding: ATR-based sizing: Stop = ATR * multiplier (typically 2-3), Size = Risk_Budget / (ATR * Multiplier). Ensures constant dollar risk regardless of volatility. Kelly fraction = (win_prob * payoff - loss_prob) / payoff. Practitioners use half-Kelly (12.5%) or quarter-Kelly (6.25%) to reduce drawdowns.
findings.relevance_to_us: HIGH. Current system uses fixed budget per trade. ATR-based sizing would automatically reduce size in volatile periods and increase in calm periods. Kelly criterion can be computed from our per-ticker accuracy data.

findings.finding: XGBoost + SHAP feature importance analysis reveals which features actually predict returns. Key finding: primary price-based features consistently outperform technical indicators in high-frequency contexts. Feature importance methods disagree - built-in vs SHAP can rank differently.
findings.relevance_to_us: MEDIUM. Could use XGBoost to learn which of our 33 active signals actually predict returns. However, requires careful cross-validation to avoid overfitting with 33 features on limited samples.

findings.finding: Springer paper (2026): PPO reinforcement learning to dynamically optimize weights of multiple LLM-generated alphas. Achieves higher Sharpe ratios and smaller max drawdowns vs static weighting, though not always highest cumulative returns.
findings.relevance_to_us: LOW. Interesting but too complex for our infrastructure. Simpler IC/accuracy-based weighting gets 80% of the benefit with 20% of the complexity.

recommended_improvements.title: IC-Based Signal Weighting
recommended_improvements.description: Replace equal voting with accuracy-proportional weighting. Weight each signal by its rolling 7-day IC at the relevant horizon. Signals with IC < 0.05 get zero weight.

recommended_improvements.title: ATR-Based Position Sizing
recommended_improvements.description: Replace fixed budget per trade with ATR-scaled position sizing. Size = RiskBudget / (ATR * 2.5). For leveraged certificates, use underlying ATR * leverage. Apply half-Kelly ceiling.

recommended_improvements.title: Horizon-Gated Signal Activation
recommended_improvements.description: Gate each signal to its best-performing horizon. Funding rate: only active at 3h (74.2%) not 1d (31.3%). Calendar: emphasize 3h (67.9%).

recommended_improvements.title: Disable Fibonacci Signal
recommended_improvements.description: Fibonacci is 43.6% at 1d and 43.3% at 3h with 17K+ samples. Consistently below coin flip. Should be disabled.

recommended_improvements.title: Walk-Forward Signal Reweighting
recommended_improvements.description: Weekly automated recalculation of signal weights based on trailing 7-day accuracy. Use existing accuracy_cache infrastructure.
